title: Depth-2 nested derivative/gradient that CAPTURES a local or vector-ref value returns garbage (~2.2e13)
goal: Found by the depth-parametric AD oracle (P6a), verified on -r AND AOT (identical). A single (depth-1) derivative whose body multiplies by a captured local-param or vector-ref value is CORRECT, but nesting it to depth 2 with the SAME capture returns a huge GARBAGE number (~2.2e13) instead of the true second derivative. capnone and GLOBAL captures at depth 2 are fine, so the defect is specific to a captured non-global factor flowing through the SECOND forward-tangent level. Minimal repro (tests/ad_depth/found/esh_0122_capture_nested_deriv_garbage.esk): d1 = ((lambda(c)(derivative (lambda(b)(* c b b b b b b b b)) 2.0)) 1.7) = 1740.8 CORRECT; d2-local = ((lambda(c)(derivative (lambda(a)(derivative (lambda(b)(* c b b b b b b b b)) a)) 2.0)) 1.7) = ~2.2e13 WRONG (expected 6092.8); d2-vecref (capture via (vref pv 0)) also garbage; d2-capnone = 3584 CORRECT. Note: this garbage-at-depth-2 is a DIFFERENT failure mode from ESH-0118 (which returns exact 0 at depth>=3 with NO capture) — here the capture corrupts the tangent already at depth 2.
detection_gap: P3 oracle tested captures only at nesting depth 1. P6a sweeps capture x depth: scripts/gen_ad_depth.py deriv.*.localparam / deriv.*.vecref and gradn.*.vecref cells now cover depth 2+, tracked by the ad-depth gate baseline (max-correct-depth 1).
acceptance: depth-2 derivative capturing a local-param returns 6092.8 (-r AND AOT); vector-ref capture likewise; capnone/global unaffected; oracle deriv.mono.s.inline.localparam / .vecref and gradn.mono.s.inline.vecref reach max-correct-depth >= 2; no regression to ad_oracle / mixed_mode / SICP
